reasoning: This market asks if Tyler Robinson (the shooter of Charlie Kirk) was a lone actor, resolving to Yes if confirmed he did not work with others in planning/perpetrating the act. The search reveals widespread reporting in September and October that law enforcement, including the police and the FBI, believe Robinson acted alone. Conspiracy theories and claims by right-wing media figures like Steve Bannon of a "second shooter" or "network of people" have been characterized by mainstream sources and law enforcement as "baseless theories" and "false claims". Further, an October 16 article directly affirms that police say he acted alone. At that same point in time (mid-October 2025), the Polymarket price was resting at 98%. The only caveat is an investigation into whether anyone in his network knew about the attack beforehand (e.g. through Discord messages), but knowledge beforehand does not equate to "working with others in planning and/or perpetrating this act", especially when authorities actively and continually assert he acted alone. Therefore, the likelihood of resolving YES is very high.
